2010-05-01.title: Changing How Qatar Sees the Blind
2010-05-01.abstract: The director of a school for the blind in Qatar, Hayat Khalil Hassan Nazar Heji, counts herself lucky despite losing her sight suddenly as a child.

2010-05-01.title: NATO Investigates 3 Afghan Civilian Deaths
2010-05-01.abstract: An Afghan official said “foreign forces” gunned down two women and a girl in southeastern Afghanistan on Friday.

2010-05-01.title: France: Ex-Minister Convicted
2010-05-01.abstract: Senator Charles Pasqua, a onetime close ally of Jacques Chirac, received a one-year suspended prison sentence for embezzlement of public funds in a case linked to the sale of police equipment abroad.

2010-05-01.title: China: World Expo Opens
2010-05-01.abstract: Shanghai kicked off the 2010 World Expo with an extravagant opening ceremony and fireworks show and a pledge to be more open and inclusive.

2010-05-01.title: U.N. Official Fears Congo Is Overcome by Violence
2010-05-01.abstract: Eastern Congo is stuck in a rut of violence, and the United Nations is concerned that donors and Western governments are weary of the bad news.

2010-05-02.title: U.N. Says Congo Rebels Killed Scores in Village
2010-05-02.abstract: Officials said the Lord’s Resistance Army rebel force killed up to 100 people in a previously unreported massacre in a remote corner of the country.

2010-05-02.title: Message Battle Heats Up in Thai Crisis
2010-05-02.abstract: Viewers of Thai soap operas now have a choice: they can follow the weepy plot lines, or they can read political messages on the bottom of the screen.

2010-05-02.title: Bombs Hit School Buses in North Iraq
2010-05-02.abstract: An Iraqi was killed and about 70 college students were wounded on the outskirts of Mosul.

2010-05-02.title: Afghans Die in Bombing, as Toll Rises for Civilians
2010-05-02.abstract: Seven people were killed by a roadside bomb, after a month of fighting in which a third more civilians were killed than in the same period in 2009.

2010-05-02.title: U.S. Is Pushing to Deter a Mideast Nuclear Race
2010-05-02.abstract: As a U.N. nuclear conference opens Monday, the U.S. worries that if Iran gets a bomb, other nations will follow.

2010-05-03.title: Election Victories Help Kurds in Iraq Push for More Sovereignty
2010-05-03.abstract: With more seats in Parliament, the president of Kurdistan seems to have new political strength behind his demands for a federalist approach to governing Iraq.

2010-05-03.title: Deflation Could Stall Efforts to Revive Greece
2010-05-03.abstract: There are serious questions about whether the harsh austerity measures Greece committed to are sustainable.

2010-05-03.title: Abuse Case Offers a View of the Vatican’s Politics
2010-05-03.abstract: The abuse case against one of the most powerful priests in the Roman Catholic Church remained stalled for years.

2010-05-03.title: British Conservative Raids Turfs of Opponents
2010-05-03.abstract: If Britain is to avoid political uncertainty after the election, it may depend on David Cameron’s forays into the political backyards of his rivals.

2010-05-03.title: Insurgents’ Seizure of a Pirate Base in Somalia Raises Questions About Its Future
2010-05-03.abstract: The fall of Xarardheere raised fears about whether rebels with connections to Al Qaeda will now have a pipeline to tens of millions of dollars — and a new ability to threaten global trade.

2010-05-04.title: Famine Persists in Niger, but Denial Is Past
2010-05-04.abstract: Once again Niger is facing a food crisis, but this time, the new military government is acknowledging serious hunger, trying to do something about it — and asking for help.

2010-05-04.title: U.S. to Send Trainers to Afghanistan as Stopgap
2010-05-04.abstract: The 850 Americans will help fill vacancies in the high-priority effort to train local security forces until European allies can provide more military personnel.

2010-05-04.title: Iraqi Sunnis Frustrated as Awakening Loses Clout
2010-05-04.abstract: The Sunni Awakening, a once-promising movement, has been weakened by internal divisions and outside threats.

2010-05-04.title: France: Synagogue Defaced and Jewish Man Is Attacked
2010-05-04.abstract: Three attackers defaced a synagogue in Nîmes on Sunday, then threw tear gas at a 78-year-old Jewish man, the local police said Monday.

2010-05-04.title: France: Sentence Is Suspended for Former Interior Minister
2010-05-04.abstract: A court last week found Charles Pasqua, 83, guilty of complicity in embezzling public funds, but suspended his one-year sentence on the basis of his age and “his past in service of France.”

2010-05-05.title: Kyrgyzstan Opens an Inquiry Into Fuel Sales to a U.S. Base
2010-05-05.abstract: Prosecutors are looking into whether the government of former President Kurmanbek Bakiyev broke the law in granting tax breaks that benefited his son.

2010-05-05.title: Liberal Democrat Relishes Central Role in Race
2010-05-05.abstract: Nick Clegg is the leader of the newly insurgent party that has thrown a wrench into conventional British politics as the country prepares for its general election on Thursday.

2010-05-05.title: Immigration Could Sway Coming Vote in Britain
2010-05-05.abstract: Opponents of large-scale immigration now include earlier generations of Muslim, black and Asian immigrants.

2010-05-05.title: Iran’s Leader Says Sanctions May Ruin Relations
2010-05-05.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad also said the U.N. atomic agency had no authority over missiles.

2010-05-05.title: Fears That a Lush Land May Lose a Foul Fertilizer
2010-05-05.abstract: Farmers are worried that treated wastewater may strip away some of the natural fertilizers that have helped make Mezquital Valley in Mexico so productive.

2010-05-06.title: A Campus Where Unlearning Is First
2010-05-06.abstract: At American University in Cairo, students are taught to analyze and hypothesize, a method at odds with the country’s traditional method of rote learning.

2010-05-06.title: As Britain Votes, Economic Clouds Hover
2010-05-06.abstract: With government deficits a rising concern, whoever wins the general election will be forced to make deep and unpopular cuts.

2010-05-06.title: Cardinal Has a Mixed Record on Sexual Abuse Cases
2010-05-06.abstract: The record of Cardinal William J. Levada, whose Vatican office oversees abuse cases, shows that he often did not act as assertively as he could have.

2010-05-06.title: President of Nigeria Dies After Long Illness
2010-05-06.abstract: President Umaru Yar’Adua did not transfer power when he left the country for emergency treatment in Saudi Arabia and Nigeria was left in limbo.

2010-05-06.title: A Visit to Faisal Shahzad's Village
2010-05-06.abstract: Adam B. Ellick visits the family village in Pakistan of the Times Square bomb suspect, where relatives discuss his upbringing and dismiss claims of his involvement in the bombing attempt as an American plot to justify the Afghan war.

2010-05-07.title: Head of Atomic Agency Asserts Right to Scrutinize Iran
2010-05-07.abstract: Yukiya Amano said that Iran would not be able to satisfy the world that its nuclear program was peaceful unless it answered a series of questions.

2010-05-07.title: ‘Son of Babylon’ Opens in Baghdad
2010-05-07.abstract: Iraqis attended the premiere of an Iraqi-made film starring Iraqi actors at the historic Semiramis Cinema.

2010-05-07.title: In a Baghdad Cinema, Celebrating a Return of Iraqi Culture
2010-05-07.abstract: At the premiere for “Son of Babylon,” excitement and relief that culture could defy occupation, war, government neglect and occasional hostility to signal its survival.

2010-05-07.title: Surprising Guests in a Russian Parade: American Troops
2010-05-07.abstract: Never before in history have active-duty American troops been invited to march in the Victory Day parade, marking the 65th anniversary of Nazi Germany’s defeat.

2010-05-07.title: The Road Ahead Is Uncertain and Likely Difficult
2010-05-07.abstract: With the British vote ending in a hung Parliament for the first time since the 1970s, several possibilities could now play out.

2010-05-08.title: Unclear Result in Britain Puts Focus on Electoral Rules
2010-05-08.abstract: The election highlighted voters’ disillusionment with an increasingly creaky electoral system and thrust a party that supports change into a pivotal role.

2010-05-08.title: Uncertainty in British Vote Adds to Economic Jitters
2010-05-08.abstract: In Britain, stocks slid by 4 percent, the pound dropped and risk premiums on 10-year bonds touched a 12-year high.

2010-05-08.title: U.S. Orchestra Performs in China, in Echoes of 1973
2010-05-08.abstract: The Philadelphia Symphony Orchestra’s latest diplomatic mission to China mirrors its visit to Beijing three years after the end of the Cultural Revolution.

2010-05-08.title: Greek Debt Crisis Raises Doubts About the European Union
2010-05-08.abstract: Experts say the union is subject to delays because it has no single government, no single treasury, no effective fiscal coordination and no mechanism for crisis management.

2010-05-08.title: Exposing Graft, but His Motives Are Murky
2010-05-08.abstract: A high-ranking Indonesian police official, himself accused of taking bribes, has been exposing government corruption and threatening to reveal more.

2010-05-09.title: Muddy Road Molds Debate on the Future of Guyana
2010-05-09.abstract: The argument over a plan to pave the road reflects a nation pulled between competing desires to delve into the global economy or pursue a more ecologically sustainable path toward development.

2010-05-09.title: Twin Explosions at a Siberian Mine Kill 12 and Trap Scores
2010-05-09.abstract: Twin methane gas explosions killed 12 coal miners and trapped 80 underground in western Siberia on Sunday.

2010-05-09.title: Aquino Son One to Watch in Philippine Elections
2010-05-09.abstract: Senator Benigno S. Aquino III, the son of two democracy icons, was favored to win in the presidential election.

2010-05-09.title: Webber Dominates Race, Schumacher Provides Show
2010-05-09.abstract: Mark Webber took his first victory of the season for Red Bull at the Spanish Grand Prix, while Michael Schumacher finished fourth in his best race of the season.

2010-05-09.title: U.S.-Brokered Mideast Shuttle Talks Begin Again
2010-05-09.abstract: The Obama administration said indirect, U.S.-brokered talks had resumed between Israel and the Palestinians.

2010-05-10.title: British Government in Limbo as Parties Bargain
2010-05-10.abstract: Gordon Brown, the Labour prime minister, was still holding on to power, despite leading his party to a loss against the Conservatives.

2010-05-10.title: Where Iraq Meets Iran, Guards See Shifting Lines
2010-05-10.abstract: At a border outpost, there is little obvious tension between Americans and Iranians, but some cold-war-style gamesmanship is evident.

2010-05-10.title: Conservatives in Germany Suffer Defeat at Polls
2010-05-10.abstract: The strong setback to the party of Chancellor Angela Merkel was seen as the first significant fallout from the Greece crisis.

2010-05-10.title: Divorce Trade Fair Shows Changing Italian Culture
2010-05-10.abstract: The latest seismic transformation of Italian society was evident in the country’s first trade fair on divorce, held over the weekend.

2010-05-10.title: Iran Executes Five Activists, Sending Message to Critics
2010-05-10.abstract: The executions were widely seen as intended to discourage people from staging anti-government rallies on June 12.

2010-05-11.title: Financial Crisis Tests Germany’s Ability to Lead
2010-05-11.abstract: Questions have been raised about Chancellor Angela Merkel’s role at a defining moment for the European Union, with Greece in financial turmoil and the euro currency troubled.

2010-05-11.title: Israel: 2 Israeli Arab Activists Arrested
2010-05-11.abstract: The activists have been charged with spying and making contact with Hezbollah of Lebanon.

2010-05-11.title: Turkey: Opposition Leader Resigns
2010-05-11.abstract: Deniz Baykal stepped down after an Internet video appeared to show him in a bedroom with a female lawmaker from his party.

2010-05-11.title: China: Murder Victim Turns Up Alive, and a Prisoner Becomes a Free Man
2010-05-11.abstract: The two men had had a fight, the authorities later found a headless body in a village well, and Zhao Zuohai confessed to the killing.

2010-05-11.title: Debt Aid Package for Europe Took Nudge From Washington
2010-05-11.abstract: President Obama advised German Chancellor Angela Merkel that Europe needed to try something big.

2010-05-12.title: Egyptian Emergency Law Is Extended for 2 Years
2010-05-12.abstract: The law, in place for almost three decades, gives the government the right to arrest people without charge and to limit freedom of expression and assembly.

2010-05-12.title: Senators Demand Tighter Rules on No-Fly List and Addition to Terror Group List
2010-05-12.abstract: A Senate Intelligence Committee briefing on the Times Square bombing attempt places new focus on the Pakistani Taliban.

2010-05-12.abstract: A colossal, two-mile-long bridge is about to be built across the vineyards of the bucolic Mosel Valley, dividing the land and the winemakers who own it.

2010-05-12.title: Cameron Faces Challenges Beyond His Coalition
2010-05-12.abstract: David Cameron’s pragmatism and flexibility will be assets as he contends with the demands of his own party and his Liberal Democratic partners.

2010-05-12.title: The Path of the Jaguar
2010-05-12.abstract: Animal conservation groups are working to establish pathways for big cats to travel through human-populated areas. One such corridor is in place for Costa Rica's jaguars.

2010-05-13.title: Afghans’ Distrust Threatens U.S. War Strategy
2010-05-13.abstract: The fate of the new U.S. strategy may well depend on whether Afghans can overcome their corrosive distrust of President Hamid Karzai’s government.

2010-05-13.abstract: After an appeal from a wounded boy, Israel may finally be set to remove most of the 260,000 land mines lining its borders.

2010-05-13.title: Somalia: U.N. Revamps Food Program
2010-05-13.abstract: The United Nations team in Somalia is developing a database of all service providers and revising its control system for the delivery of aid and cash transfers, according to the latest council report.

2010-05-13.title: China: Confession Obtained by Torture
2010-05-13.abstract: Officials in the city of Shangqiu in Henan Province have admitted that police officers tortured a man to get him to confess to a murder that he did not commit.

2010-05-13.abstract: On the second anniversary of the Sichuan earthquake, a prominent Chinese artist posted an Internet audio project in which volunteers read the names of nearly 5,000 children who were killed.

2010-05-14.abstract: The notion that the government can execute an American-born cleric far from a combat zone, with no judicial process, has set off a debate among authorities.

2010-05-14.title: In India, Hitching Hopes on a Subway

2010-05-14.abstract: The plan to spend $80 billion over 10 years could help win Republican support for his new arms control treaty with Russia.

2010-05-14.abstract: New Delhi’s subway system, known as the Metro, is on track to finish its 118-mile network by fall, right on schedule and within its $6.55 billion budget.

2010-05-14.abstract: By playing host to much of President Hamid Karzai’s cabinet, the U.S. seeks to cultivate Afghan technocrats.

2010-05-15.title: In South Africa, an Unlikely Leader on AIDS
2010-05-15.abstract: President Jacob Zuma has been criticized for his personal behavior, but his anti-AIDS program is drawing praise.

2010-05-15.abstract: Military commanders worry that an area in the north offers a glimpse of a post-American Sunni insurgency.

2010-05-15.abstract: Every 10 years since 1634, a village has put on a Passion play, but this year church scandals have shaken the faith of some actors.

2010-05-15.abstract: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva of Brazil will try to persuade President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran to temper his nuclear ambitions.

2010-05-15.abstract: With estimates that the government may be losing $30 billion a year to tax dodgers, the nation named 57 doctors believed guilty of tax offenses.

2010-05-16.abstract: A judge charged Mohamed Saif ur-Rehman Khan with illegal possession of explosives, but released him after finding no evidence linking him to terrorist networks.

2010-05-16.abstract: A rogue general who was shot by a sniper last week died Monday from his wounds, as clashes raged in Bangkok.

2010-05-17.abstract: The farmers’ departure is an indication that Taliban fighters have found a way to resume their insurgency in Marja.

2010-05-17.abstract: The attacks, at a market and a bus station in Kigali, came amid rising political tensions before a presidential election in August.

2010-05-17.title: Sign of Afghan Addiction May Also Be Its Remedy
2010-05-17.abstract: All of the patients at the Hospital for Interior Ministry Addicts  are policemen.

2010-05-18.title: Moscow Journalists Under Attack
2010-05-18.abstract: In the Moscow suburbs, freedom of the press may be legal, but it's not always tolerated.

2010-05-18.abstract: The interrogation Pyotr Lipatov a year after he was attacked represents a telling example of the way the authorities put intense pressure on journalists.

2010-05-18.abstract: Atta Muhammad Noor, governor of Balkh Province, is credited with promoting development there.

2010-05-18.abstract: Mazar-i-Sharif, a northern provincial city in Afghanistan, has distinguished itself as one of the most secure places in the country.

2010-05-18.title: Russian Journalists, Fighting Graft, Pay in Blood
2010-05-18.abstract: Journalists and activists are being battered in a wave of unsolved attacks, a violent deterrent to those who question authority.

2010-05-19.abstract: Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky declared a hunger strike to protest a court decision that extended his term in custody while he stood trial for large-scale money laundering and embezzlement.

2010-05-19.abstract: Bulldozers, accompanied by Hamas forces, razed at least 25 houses in Gaza.

2010-05-19.title: Killing Taints Iraqi Kurdistan’s Image
2010-05-19.abstract: The slaying of a journalist is underscoring the limits of free expression and igniting debate about what issues could cost others their lives.

2010-05-19.abstract: The immigration case thrust the new coalition government of Conservatives and Liberal Democrats into an issue that starkly exposes their differing approaches to a major policy issue.

2010-05-19.abstract: Antigovernment leaders pleaded with their followers to stand down after army troops launched a push to take over the protesters’ encampment in Bangkok.

2010-05-20.abstract: Two people were killed and 62 were wounded in street fighting around the People’s Friendship University, according to the Kyrgyz Health Ministry.

2010-05-20.title: A Bitter Spring for Mongolia's Nomads
2010-05-20.abstract: A drought last summer followed by an unusually cold winter has killed more than eight million animals, about 17 percent of the country’s livestock.

2010-05-20.abstract: Cold and drought have ravaged Mongolian grasslands, killing millions of livestock and the country’s economy.

2010-05-20.title: Diplomatic Storm Brewing Over Korean Peninsula
2010-05-20.abstract: South Korea is expected to ask the United Nations to review its charge that North Korea sank one of its ships, complicating a trip to China by a delegation of senior American officials.

2010-05-20.abstract: What were the names of those uninvited White House guests again? Forgotten, White House officials hoped, in the mists of Washington trivia.

2010-05-21.abstract: The United States on Thursday dispatched the first of a flotilla of senior officials to China for high-level economic and security meetings.

2010-05-21.abstract: Russia’s Paralympic team is the world’s dominant, an impressive feat when considering that many disabled people here have a hard time just getting out the front door.

2010-05-21.title: Repression Is Alleged Before Vote in Ethiopia
2010-05-21.abstract: Diplomats, human rights groups and witnesses say the government is stifling dissent ahead of national elections.

2010-05-21.abstract: Abdullah Abdullah is getting none of the V.I.P. treatment accorded to his rival, the Afghan president, Hamid Karzai, during a visit to a Washington wary of antagonizing Mr. Karzai.

2010-05-21.abstract: Britain’s new prime minister, David Cameron, traveled to Paris to meet with President Nicolas Sarkozy before going to Germany to meet with Chancellor Angela Merkel.

2010-05-22.title: U.S. Makes Concessions to Russia for Iran Sanctions
2010-05-22.abstract: The Obama administration lifted American sanctions against the Russian military complex and agreed not to ban the sale of Russian anti-aircraft batteries to Tehran.

2010-05-22.title: France: Paris Mayor Demands Investigation Into Art Theft
2010-05-22.abstract: Mayor Bertrand Delanoë of Paris demanded an internal investigation into this week’s theft of five paintings from the Paris Museum of Modern Art.

2010-05-22.abstract: Both politicians and militants say the structures that propel the Pakistani insurgency are embedded in Karachi.

2010-05-22.title: Iran Presses Opposition With Attacks and Jail Time
2010-05-22.abstract: A former vice president and a filmmaker, once insiders in the Islamic republic, were attacked, and student leaders received long prison terms as the anniversary of the contested election approached.

2010-05-22.title: Somalia: Radio Station Attacked
2010-05-22.abstract: The Islamist insurgent group Al Shabab invaded the Boondheere neighborhood, killing 3 people, wounding at least 12 more and looting a local radio station of its equipment.

2010-05-23.title: Japan Relents on U.S. Base on Okinawa
2010-05-23.abstract: Japan’s prime minister, Yukio Hatoyama, reneged on a campaign promise to move the U.S. air base off Okinawa.

2010-05-23.title: U.S. Presses China to Punish North Korea for Ship
2010-05-23.abstract: The U.S. began pressing China to back punitive measures against North Korea but U.S. officials acknowledged the Chinese were reluctant.

2010-05-23.title: Heartland of Thailand, Still Angry and Divided
2010-05-23.abstract: After a terrifying military action in which 15 people were killed, “red shirt” protesters are arriving home to a more hardened, divided and angry society.

2010-05-23.title: On North Korea, China Prefers Fence
2010-05-23.abstract: Chinese leaders face two unpalatable options: mollify North Korea or join the U.N. Security Council in condemning it.

2010-05-23.title: No Worries, Israel Insists, Defense Drill Is Just a Drill
2010-05-23.abstract: As Israel embarked on a large-scale civil defense exercise, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu sought to reassure Israelis and some jittery Arab neighbors.

2010-05-24.title: An Afghan Reconciliation
2010-05-24.abstract: In Mian Poshteh, Afghanistan, Elisabeth Bumiller sits in on a shura, where Marines and village elders attempt to reintegrate a former Taliban insurgent.

2010-05-24.abstract: A tabloid said Sarah Ferguson took a $40,000 down payment for pledging an introduction to Prince Andrew, her ex-husband.

2010-05-24.abstract: The judge who issued the arrest warrant said Father Marcin Michael Strachanowski had used his parish’s rectory as an “erotic dungeon” to carry out sex acts with boys.

2010-05-24.title: U.S. Tries to Reintegrate Taliban Soldiers
2010-05-24.abstract: Even as officials debate how to reconcile with members of the Taliban, military commanders on the ground in Afghanistan are proceeding on their own.

2010-05-24.abstract: Ethiopians went to the polls on Sunday to choose a new Parliament, with Prime Minister Meles Zenawi’s ruling party expected to sweep the vote and extend its 19-year rule.

2010-05-25.title: Climate Fears Turn to Doubts Among Britons
2010-05-25.abstract: In Britain, climate change skeptics have dominated news reports, convincing many that the threat is exaggerated.

2010-05-25.title: British Medical Council Bars Doctor Who Linked Vaccine With Autism
2010-05-25.abstract: Dr. Andrew Wakefield was banned for ethical lapses, including conducting invasive medical procedures on children that they did not need.

2010-05-25.title: Russia: Ex-Premier Accuses Putin in Tycoon’s Trial
2010-05-25.abstract: Former Prime Minister Mikhail Kasyanov said Vladimir V. Putin, then Russia’s president, ordered the prosecution of an oil tycoon because he was supporting political parties without Mr. Putin’s permission.

2010-05-25.title: U.S. Is Said to Expand Secret Actions in Mideast
2010-05-25.abstract: A secret directive by Gen. David H. Petraeus is aimed at disrupting militant groups and countering other threats.

2010-05-25.title: Iran Deal Seen as Spot on Brazilian Leader’s Legacy
2010-05-25.abstract: After signing a deal to exchange uranium, President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva returned home to criticism that the mission was naïve or even damaging to Brazil.

2010-05-26.title: Former Exile Holds Power in West African Nation
2010-05-26.abstract: The American government sees Rear Adm. José Américo Bubo Na Tchuto as a trafficking mastermind in Bissau, a narcotics hub.

2010-05-26.title: Chinese Economy Treads Risky Path. (Ask Japan.)
2010-05-26.abstract: China has grown from a peasant nation into an economic colossus, but sustaining such robust expansion may be difficult.

2010-05-26.title: Former Attorney General in Canada Is Cleared in Traffic Death
2010-05-26.abstract: A special prosecutor dropped all charges against Michael Bryant stemming from a traffic altercation last summer in Toronto in which a bicycle messenger died.

2010-05-26.title: Premier’s Party Sweeps Ethiopian Vote
2010-05-26.abstract: Provisional results from the country’s election board show that Prime Minister Meles Zenawi’s governing party won nearly every seat in Sunday’s parliamentary vote.

2010-05-26.title: Peru Frees American Held Since 1995
2010-05-26.abstract: Lori Berenson, the 40-year-old New Yorker who spent the last 15 years in prison in Peru, was paroled.

2010-05-27.title: Russian Knights Joust to Control Chess World
2010-05-27.abstract: A conflict has pitted two former world champions and onetime enemies, Garry Kasparov and Anatoly Karpov, against high-ranking Russian officials.

2010-05-27.title: A Case Built in New York Against a Jamaican Kingpin
2010-05-27.abstract: Court records suggest how drug dealing in New York might have helped fortify Christopher Coke’s stronghold in Jamaica.

2010-05-27.title: For Peru and American Inmate, Much Is Changed
2010-05-27.abstract: Now that Lori Berenson has been granted parole after 14 years in a Peruvian prison on terrorism charges, both she and the nation that imprisoned her have changed.

2010-05-27.title: Polish Leader’s Mother Finally Learns of His Death
2010-05-27.abstract: Six weeks after a deadly plane crash, Jaroslaw Kaczynski, the brother of the dead president, informed their invalid mother of the catastrophe.

2010-05-27.title: France: Rwandan Doctor Arrested and Accused in ’94 Genocide
2010-05-27.abstract: The doctor, Eugene Rwamucyo, is suspected of being involved in the 1994 genocide in Rwanda, was arrested.

2010-05-28.title: Police Department Puts Corpulent Cops on a Diet
2010-05-28.abstract: To slim down a force in which three-quarters of the more than 70,000 officers are considered overweight, the powers that be have imposed a rather austere diet at official department eateries.

2010-05-28.title: Oxford Tradition Comes to This: ‘Death’ (Expound)
2010-05-28.abstract: All Souls College is scrapping its one-word exam, on which applicants demonstrated their intellectual flexibility.

2010-05-28.title: Report Outlines Abuse Claims at German Jesuit Schools
2010-05-28.abstract: The report on Thursday by a special investigator said that 205 former students claimed they had been abused in the schools, deepening the sexual abuse crisis in the Roman Catholic Church in Germany.

2010-05-28.title: Downturn Does Little to Slow Migration
2010-05-28.abstract: While migrants have encountered resistance from the United States to South Africa, their numbers appear undiminished; one nation in particular, the Philippines, promotes its workers abroad.

2010-05-28.title: U.N. Official to Ask U.S. to End C.I.A. Drone Strikes
2010-05-28.abstract: The official will argue that the strikes should be entrusted to regular armed forces, not intelligence agencies.

2010-05-29.title: Nepal Avoids Crisis in Deal to Extend Parliament
2010-05-29.abstract: The agreement allows more time for the peace process and stipulates that the prime minister will resign at some point.

2010-05-29.title: South Africa: Paper Apologizes for Cartoon of Muhammad
2010-05-29.abstract: The Mail & Guardian said Friday that it had underestimated “the depth of anger ignited” by a cartoon it published that depicted the Prophet Muhammad lying on a psychiatrist’s couch.

2010-05-29.title: Haiti: Ex-Director of Prison Is Taken Into Custody
2010-05-29.abstract: The former director of the prison in Les Cayes, where a dozen or more inmates were shot dead on Jan. 19, was taken into custody by the judicial police, according to a United Nations official.

2010-05-29.title: When Afghans Seek Medical Aid, Tough Choice for U.S.
2010-05-29.abstract: Civilians wounded as a result of military activity are treated by the military. But each day, Afghans seek help for other ailments.

2010-05-29.title: Jamaica Accused of Brutality in Hunting Suspect
2010-05-29.abstract: A growing number of Kingston residents claim security forces used excessive force in battling the backers of a wanted gang leader.

2010-05-30.title: Reaching Out
2010-05-30.abstract: Female Marines in Helmand Province have the mission of winning over the Afghan women who are culturally off limits to American men.

2010-05-30.title: China Balks at Criticism of North Korea
2010-05-30.abstract: Prime Minister Wen Jiabao said Sunday that China’s top priority was still ensuring stability and avoiding tensions and a possible military clash between the two Koreas.

2010-05-30.title: Two McLarens Top Race as Two Red Bulls Collide
2010-05-30.abstract: As Sebastian Vettel tried to pass his teammate Mark Webber at Red Bull, the two collided and handed the race lead and eventual victory to Lewis Hamilton at McLaren and second place to his teammate, Jenson Button.

2010-05-30.title: NATO Has High Hopes for Afghan Peace Council
2010-05-30.abstract: Western leaders are banking on a national peace council to start a new chapter in Afghan politics.

2010-05-30.title: A Tragic Day for a Faith Under Siege
2010-05-30.abstract: An author fears that a recent attack on Ahmadis in Pakistan won’t be the last simply because religious extremists don’t accept them as Muslims.

2010-05-31.title: Georgia President Aided by Mayoral Election
2010-05-31.abstract: Early exit poll results gave an overwhelming lead to the capital city’s incumbent mayor, Gigi Ugulava, a longtime political ally of President Mikheil Saakashvili.

2010-05-31.title: Israel Intercepts Gaza Flotilla; Violence Reported
2010-05-31.abstract: Television reports early Monday said Israeli forces had attacked ships carrying activists and supplies to Gaza, killing at least two people on board.

2010-05-31.title: U.S. to Aid South Korea With Naval Defense Plan
2010-05-31.abstract: The sinking of a warship revealed that South Korea is still vulnerable to a surprise attack, U.S. officials say.

2010-05-31.title: Afghan Girls Flogged for Running Away
2010-05-31.abstract: In a remote village in central Afghanistan, a former warlord publicly flogged two girls for running away from the husbands they had been illegally forced to marry.

2010-05-31.title: U.S. Presses Pakistan for More Data on Travelers
2010-05-31.abstract: The Obama administration wants broader airline passenger information to track terrorist travel patterns.
